commit.message:
fix: print system — gebruik directe URLs + popup ipv broken hidden iframe/blob

Print werkte niet omdat:
- Blob URLs in hidden iframes worden geblokkeerd door browser PDF plugins
- iframe.contentWindow.print() faalt op blob PDF URLs
- print:hidden class verbergde PDF bij window.print() fallback

Fix:
- print-preview: directe API URL als iframe src (same-origin, printbaar)
- print-queue: opent print-preview als popup window ipv hidden iframe
- Queue wacht tot popup sluit, dan volgende job (sequentieel)
- Gebruiker ziet PDF preview en print dialog (visuele feedback)

commit.message:
Improve Claude analysis logging and fix Legacy model issue

Issue: All Claude analysis was failing with:
"Access denied. This Model is marked by provider as Legacy and you
have not been actively using the model in the last 15 days."

Changes:
1. Changed default model from us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0
   to anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0 (stable, widely available)

2. Enhanced logging visibility:
   ✓ Success: "✓ Claude analysis succeeded for: {file}"
   ✗ Failure: "✗ Claude analysis failed for {file}, using fallback summary"
   → Disabled: "→ Using simple summary (Claude disabled) for: {file}"

3. Show Bedrock errors at INFO level (not just DEBUG):
   - Users can now see "Access denied", "Model not found", etc.
   - Full stack trace still at DEBUG level

4. Log unsupported mime types:
   - Shows count of files with unsupported mime types
   - Examples at DEBUG level

5. Log when no text is extracted from a file

Now users can see at a glance:
- Which files succeeded with Claude analysis
- Which files failed and why (Bedrock error visible)
- Which files used fallback summaries
- Which files have unsupported mime types

Example log output:
  Skipping 5 files with unsupported mime types for content analysis
  ✓ Claude analysis succeeded for: Report_2024.pdf
  ✗ Claude analysis failed for: Letter.docx, using fallback summary
  Claude via Bedrock failed for Letter.docx: Access denied. This Model...

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v3: fix depleted reading, EMA smoothing, boot calibration screen

Root causes diagnosed from ESP32-C6 BSP bug reports:

1. Remove analogReadResolution(12) — documented bug on ESP32-C6 causes
   analogReadMilliVolts() to return half the true value when called after
   analogReadResolution(); 12-bit is the hardware default so the call is
   both redundant and harmful (gh issues #8999, #11041, #11324).

2. Change V_DIVIDER default to 2.0 — Expansion Board v1.1 uses a
   200kΩ:200kΩ (1:1) divider, giving VBAT/2 on A0. Previous code used 4.0
   which happened to partially cancel the halved ADC readings (two wrongs
   making a right). Boot calibration screen now lets the user verify.

3. Replace 8-sample average with EMA (α=0.05, τ≈3.8s) + 32-sample
   per-read average — eliminates the visible bar jumping.

4. Add 3-second boot calibration screen showing raw ADC mV so the user
   can compute the correct V_DIVIDER using a multimeter.

5. Replace voltage-inference USB heuristic with (bool)Serial — on XIAO
   ESP32-C6 Serial IS the USB CDC interface; returns true only when a
   USB host has enumerated the device. Honest about charger-only limitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolver error en petición al instalar en otra PC

- Agrega PRISMA_QUERY_ENGINE_LIBRARY al env del servidor para apuntar
  explícitamente al motor de Prisma (.dll.node) sin importar la PC
- Crea el directorio uploads/ automáticamente al iniciar la app
- Incluye biblioteca.db con la estructura de tablas ya creada para
  que la BD funcione desde la primera ejecución sin migraciones
- Corrige DATABASE_URL en .env a ruta relativa (eliminando ruta hardcodeada)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(context): git worktree support for repo revision, packed-refs fallback, cache key completeness

internal/cli/context.go:
- Add resolveGitDir() to handle git worktree checkouts where .git is a
  file containing "gitdir: <path>" instead of a directory, enabling
  correct HEAD resolution in worktree-based agent sessions
- Add readGitRevision() with packed-refs fallback for repos that have
  been gc'd — reads refs/heads/<branch> first, falls back to parsing
  packed-refs file when loose ref is missing
- Refactor getRepoRev() to use the new helpers, fixing silent "unknown"
  returns in worktree environments that caused unnecessary cache misses

internal/cli/context_inject_test.go:
- Add TestGetRepoRev_DirectoryGitDir: validates standard .git directory
- Add TestGetRepoRev_WorktreeGitDirFile: validates worktree .git file
  with relative gitdir path resolution
- Add TestGetRepoRev_PackedRefsFallback: validates packed-refs parsing
  when loose ref file is absent

internal/context/pack.go:
- Include all BuildOptions fields in cacheKey hash: Task, CorrelationID,
  ProjectDir, SessionID, Files, plus BudgetAllocation — previously only
  RepoRev/BeadID/AgentType/IncludeMSSkills were hashed, causing cache
  collisions between packs with different tasks or allocation budgets
- Use null-byte delimited writes to prevent hash collisions from field
  value concatenation (e.g., "ab"+"c" vs "a"+"bc")

internal/context/pack_test.go:
- Update all existing cacheKey() calls to pass allocation parameter
- Add tests for Task, Files, ProjectDir, SessionID, CorrelationID, and
  allocation sensitivity in cache key generation
- Add TestBuildDoesNotReuseCacheAcrossDistinctInputs: validates that
  Build() returns fresh packs when task/correlation change
- Add TestBuildDoesNotReuseCacheAcrossAllocationChanges: validates that
  different BudgetAllocation settings produce distinct cache entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: interactive Hero with vertical carousel and progressive animations

Hero Section - Two Column Interactive Layout:
- Left column: Title, description, CTA buttons, and stats
- Right column: Vertical auto-play carousel with product images
- Hero entrance animations: content from left, carousel from right

Vertical Carousel Implementation:
- GSAP-powered smooth vertical sliding animation
- 4 product images (Protein, Creatine, Pre-workout, BCAA)
- Auto-play: 3-second intervals with smooth transitions
- Dot navigation: Click to jump to specific slide
- Hover pause: Stops autoplay when hovering over carousel
- Yellow gradient overlays at top/bottom for depth
- Active dot: Yellow-400 with scale-125, inactive: zinc-700

Hero Content Features:
- "Desde 2016" badge with yellow border
- Large bold title: "NUESTRA HISTORIA" (80px desktop)
- Expanded description paragraph
- Two CTA buttons: "Conoce Más" (scroll to #story) and "Ver Productos"
- Stats grid: 8+ Years, 5K+ Clients, 100% Premium
- All yellow-400 accents for brand consistency

Progressive Scroll Animations:
1. Story section text card: Slides in from left (opacity 0->1, x -80->0)
2. Story section image: Slides in from right (opacity 0->1, x 80->0)
3. Values section title/description: Fades up with stagger (y 30->0)
4. Value cards: Staggered scale-up with bounce (back.out easing)
5. Mission section: Title and text fade up with stagger
6. CTA buttons: Bounce in from below (scale 0.9->1, back.out)

GSAP ScrollTrigger Configuration:
- All animations trigger at 75-85% viewport
- once: true - prevents re-animation on scroll back
- Smooth easing: power3.out, power2.out, back.out
- Stagger timing: 0.15-0.2s for natural progression

Animation Performance:
- will-change: transform on animated elements
- backface-visibility: hidden prevents flickering
- GPU acceleration for smooth 60fps animations
- Minimal layout reflows

Carousel Technical Details:
- Vertical positioning via GSAP y property
- Slide height calculation: offsetHeight + 24px gap
- Infinite loop: currentIndex wraps to 0 after last slide
- Click handlers on dots for direct navigation
- mouseenter/mouseleave for autoplay control

Responsive Design:
- Hero: Stacks on mobile, side-by-side on lg+
- Carousel height: 400px mobile, 600px desktop
- Buttons: Stack vertically on mobile, horizontal on sm+
- Stats: 3-column grid at all breakpoints

Visual Polish:
- Rounded corners (rounded-2xl) on carousel
- Drop shadows on product images
- Border: zinc-800 on carousel container
- Smooth scroll-behavior for anchor links
- Yellow-400 highlights throughout

Purpose:
- Immediate visual engagement with animated entrance
- Product showcase through carousel
- Clear CTAs drive user action
- Progressive disclosure keeps attention
- Professional, modern brand experience

commit.message:
fix: default startup mode to autonomous instead of calm

Personal agent should default to fully autonomous after restart.
Mode persistence already works via mode.json — this only changes
the fallback when mode.json is missing or corrupted.

Fixes the issue where CI/CD deploy → restart → mode resets to idle,
causing mushi to skip cycles and delaying behavior fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(db): ensure Failed query errors are retried and not surfaced to Sentry (JOV-1433) (#5545)

Before this fix, every retry attempt for a transient "Failed query:" error
called logDbError without the asBreadcrumb flag, causing Sentry.captureException
to fire on each intermediate attempt — including ones that would succeed on the
next try. This flooded the Sentry error dashboard with noise from Neon WebSocket
cold-start reconnections.

Fix: pass { asBreadcrumb: willRetry } so that intermediate retryable failures
are recorded as breadcrumbs only. The final unrecoverable error (after all
retries are exhausted, or for non-retryable errors) still surfaces to Sentry
via normal exception propagation from the caller.

Tests: add two new cases asserting breadcrumb-vs-exception routing for
retryable and non-retryable failures, and add the required Sentry mock.

commit.message:
Fix AWS Bedrock model invocation with configurable model ID

Issue: AWS Bedrock was failing with ValidationException:
"Invocation of model ID anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0
with on-demand throughput isn't supported."

Solution:
1. Changed default model ID to use cross-region inference profile:
   us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0

2. Added --claude-model-id parameter for users to specify:
   - Direct model IDs (e.g., anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0)
   - Cross-region inference profiles (us.*, eu.*)
   - Custom inference profile ARNs

3. Added logging to show which model is being used

Usage examples:
# Use default cross-region profile (recommended)
python clean_obsolete.py FOLDER_ID

# Use EU cross-region profile
python clean_obsolete.py FOLDER_ID --claude-model-id eu.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0

# Use older stable model
python clean_obsolete.py FOLDER_ID --claude-model-id anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0

This should resolve Claude analysis failures and allow proper content
summaries to be displayed instead of fallback first-words summaries.

commit.message:
feat(versioning): single-source-of-truth versioning with hatch-vcs (#28)

## Summary

- Replace static `version = "0.1.0"` in `pyproject.toml` with
`hatch-vcs` dynamic versioning — the git tag is now the sole version
source
- Remove `src/apicurio_serdes/_version.py` (hardcoded version) and
expose `__version__` via `importlib.metadata` instead
- Remove the `validate-version` CI job in `publish.yml` (nothing to
validate anymore)
- Remove the `sed`-based pre-release version-munging step in `ci.yml`
- Add `fetch-depth: 0` to checkouts that need full git history for
hatch-vcs to resolve the version

## How to release going forward

Just create and push the tag — no file edits needed:
```bash
git tag v0.1.1
git push origin v0.1.1
```

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes on this PR (pre-release build uses auto-generated dev
version from git state)
- [ ] Create a `v0.1.1` release tag and verify the publish workflow
completes without the `validate-version` job

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
